parameters.content:
## WF-VEP-002: Social Listening Pipeline

Flow: Webhook → Scrape 5 platforms (Reddit, Google Maps, LinkedIn, G2, Capterra) → Extract → Merge All → Filter → POST Market Intel + AI Classify (batched) → POST Pain Points

Platforms: Reddit (keyword search), Google Maps (place reviews), LinkedIn (post search), G2 (product reviews), Capterra (product reviews)

Environment vars: PORTFOLIO_BASE_URL, N8N_INGEST_SECRET

## Required Setup
1. Apify credentials
2. Anthropic credentials (HTTP Header Auth)
3. Env vars: PORTFOLIO_BASE_URL, N8N_INGEST_SECRET

assignments.name: searchTerms
assignments.value: ={{ JSON.stringify($json.body && $json.body.searchTerms ? $json.body.searchTerms : ['small business technology challenges', 'nonprofit operations software pain points', 'minority business owner automation struggles']) }}

assignments.name: linkedinKeywords
assignments.value: ={{ JSON.stringify($json.body && $json.body.linkedinKeywords ? $json.body.linkedinKeywords : ['small business automation challenges', 'nonprofit technology struggles', 'black owned business operations']) }}

parameters.customBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ searches: $json.keywords.split(',').map(k => k.trim()), sort: 'new', time: 'month', maxItems: $json.maxResults, maxPostCount: $json.maxResults, searchPosts: true, proxy: { useApifyProxy: true, apifyProxyGroups: ['RESIDENTIAL'] } }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ searchStringsArray: $json.keywords.split(',').map(k => k.trim()), maxCrawledPlacesPerSearch: Math.ceil($json.maxResults / 3), language: 'en', maxReviews: 10 }) }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ items: [{ source_platform: $json.platform, content_text: ($json.title || '') + (($json.title && $json.body) ? '\n\n' : '') + ($json.body || ''), content_type: $json.content_type === 'social_post' ? 'post' : ($json.content_type === 'review' ? 'review' : 'other'), source_url: $json.url || '', source_author: $json.author || '' }] }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514', max_tokens: 4000, messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'You are a business pain point classifier for ' + $('Set Search Parameters').first().json.industry + ' companies.

Analyze these ' + $json.itemCount + ' social media items and extract business pain points:

' + $json.contentList + '

Map each pain point to ONE of these existing categories (use the exact name):
- manual_data_entry: Time spent on repetitive data entry tasks that could be automated
- slow_response_times: Delayed responses to client inquiries leading to lost opportunities
- inconsistent_followup: Leads falling through the cracks due to lack of systematic follow-up
- scattered_tools: Using multiple disconnected tools leading to lost context and double-work
- manual_reporting: Hours spent creating reports that could be auto-generated
- poor_lead_qualification: Wasting time on unqualified leads due to lack of scoring/filtering
- knowledge_loss: Critical business knowledge lives in peoples heads, not systems
- scaling_bottlenecks: Manual processes preventing business growth beyond current capacity
- employee_onboarding: New hires take too long to become productive due to poor systems
- customer_churn: Losing customers due to reactive rather than proactive engagement

Return a JSON array of pain points found across ALL items. Each object should have:
- category: one of the exact category names listed above
- severity: high, medium, or low
- confidence_score: 0.0 to 1.0
- source_excerpt: the relevant quote (max 200 chars)
- monetary_indicator: any dollar amount or time cost mentioned (or null)

Only classify content that genuinely relates to a business pain point. Skip irrelevant content.
If no relevant pain points found in any item, return an empty array [].
Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanation.' }] }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ evidence: [{ pain_point_category_name: $json.pain_point_category_name, source_type: $json.source_type, source_id: $json.source_id, source_excerpt: $json.source_excerpt, confidence_score: $json.confidence_score, monetary_indicator: $json.monetary_indicator, monetary_context: $json.monetary_context, industry: $json.industry }] }) }}

parameters.customBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ searchQueries: JSON.parse($json.linkedinKeywords), maxPosts: $json.maxResults, postedLimit: 'month', sortBy: 'date' }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const items = $input.all();
return items;
